节点.描述: 模块头结点，源自 @kit 注释

节点.功能描述: Defines the navigation common title.
Sets the main title.
Sets the sub title.
Defines the navigation custom title.
Custom content of the title bar.
Height of the title bar.
Navigation mode
The navigation bar and the content area are displayed in stack.
The navigation bar and the content area are displayed side by side.
If the window width is greater than the sum of minNavBarWidth and minContentWidth, the navigation component is displayed in split mode. Otherwise it's displayed in stack mode.
Navigation bar position
In navigation split mode, the main column is at the start of the main axis.
The navigation bar is on the End of the container
Navigation title mode.

When the content is more than one screen in a scrollable component, the main title shrinks as the content scrolls down (the subtitle fades out with its size remaining unchanged) and restores as the content scrolls up to the top.
**NOTE**
The effect where the main title's size changes in response to content scrolling is effective only when **title** is set to **ResourceStr** or *NavigationCommonTitle**. If **title** is set to any other value type, the main title changes in mere location when pulled down.
For this effect to work when the content is less than one screen in a scrollable component, set the **options** parameter of the scrollable component's edgeEffect attribute to **true**. In the non-scrolling state, the height of the title bar is the same as in **Full** mode; in the scrolling state, the minimum height of the title bar is the same as in **Mini** mode.

The title is fixed at full mode.
Default value: If there is only a main title, the title bar height is 112 vp; if there is both a main title and a subtitle, the title bar height is 138 vp.

The title is fixed at mini mode. Default value: In versions earlier than API version 12, If there is only a main title, the title bar height is 56 vp; if there are both a main title and a subtitle, The title bar height is 82 vp. Since API version 12, the title bar height is 56 vp.
Navigation menu item, include menu icon and menu info
The value of navigation menu item.
The icon of navigation menu item.
The symbol of navigation menu item.
Whether to enable this menu item.
Trigger by navigation menu item click.
Indicates the information of the popped page.
The info of the popped page.
The result of the popped page.
Indicates the information of NavDestination.
Creates an instance of NavPathInfo. Default value: **false**. *true**: The navigation destination page is the entry page. *false**: The navigation destination page is not the entry page.
注释信息: @param { string } name - The name of NavDestination.
@param { unknown } param - The detailed parameter of the NavDestination.
@param { ?import('../api/@ohos.base').Callback<PopInfo> } onPop - The callback when next page returns.
@param { ?boolean } isEntry - Indicates whether it is an entry destination.

parameters.说明: The name of NavDestination.
The detailed parameter of the NavDestination.
The callback when next page returns.
Indicates whether it is an entry destination.

boolean } [isEntry] - Indicates whether it is an entry destination.

节点.功能描述: Name of the navigation destination page.
Detailed parameters of the navigation destination page.
The detailed parameter of the NavDestination.
Callback returned when pop is called on the navigation destination page. It is invoked only after the **result** parameter is set in pop.
The callback when next page returns.
Indicates whether it is an entry destination.
The unique id of NavDestination.
Defines the mode of stack operation.

Default navigation stack operation mode.
In this mode, push operations add the specified **NavDestination**page to the stack; replace operations replace the current top **NavDestination** page.
When the NavDestination with a specified name exists, it will be moved to top of stack, otherwise, the behavior will be consistent with the STANDARD mode.
When the NavDestination with a specified name exists, the stack will pop until that NavDestination, otherwise, the behavior will be consistent with the STANDARD mode.
This mode creates an instance of **NavDestination**. Compared with *STANDARD**, this mode does not reuse the instance with the same name in the stack.
Indicates the options of stack operation.

Navigation stack operation mode.
Default value: **LaunchMode.STANDARD**.

Whether to support transition animation.
Default value: **true**.
**true**: The transition animation is supported.
**false**: The transition animation is not supported.
Indicates the information of NavDestinations. Providers methods for controlling destination page in the stack
Creates an instance of NavPathStack.

Pushes the navigation destination page specified by **info** onto the navigation stack.
Default value: **true**.
**true**: The transition animation is supported. **false**: The transition animation is not supported.
注释信息: @param { NavPathInfo } info - Indicates the NavDestination to be pushed.
@param { boolean } [animated] - Whether to support the transition animation.

parameters.说明: Indicates the NavDestination to be pushed.
boolean } [animated] - Whether to support the transition animation.

节点.功能描述:
Pushes the navigation destination page specified by **info** onto the navigation stack. This API uses a promise to return the result.

节点.功能描述: Defines the status of toolbar item and it is used in the ToolbarItem interface.
Normal state. In this state, the toolbar item takes on the default style and can switch to another state-specific style by responding to the hover, press, and focus events.
Disable state of toolbar item.
Active state. In this state, the toolbar item can update its icon to the one specified by **activeIcon** by responding to a click event.
Defines the operation of current navigation transition.
Push operation of navigation transition.
Pop operation of navigation transition.
The transition is page replacement.
Defines configurable parameters for toolbar item.
The value of navigation toolbar item.
The icon of navigation toolbar item.
The symbol of navigation toolbar item.
Trigger by navigation toolbar item click.
The state of navigation toolbar item.
The icon of navigation toolbar item in active state.
The symbol of navigation toolbar item in active state.
Indicates the options of Navigation's Titlebar.
Background color of the title bar. If this parameter is not set, the default color is used.
Background blur style of the title bar. If this parameter is not set, the background blur effect is disabled.
Background blur style options.
Background effect options.

Layout style of the title bar.
Default value: **BarStyle.STANDARD**.
Set title bar start padding.
Set title bar end padding.
Text modifier for main title.
Text modifier for sub title.
Defines whether to respond to the hover mode.
Declare BarStyle enum.
In this mode, the title bar or toolbar is laid out above the content area.
In this mode, the title bar or toolbar is overlaid on top of the content area.
SafeAreaPadding style means the bar height will be taken as content's safeAreaPadding.
Indicates the options of Navigation's Toolbar.
Background color of the toolbar. If this parameter is not set, the default color is used.
Background blur style of the toolbar. If this parameter is not set, the background blur effect is disabled.
Background blur style options.
Background effect options.
More button options.
Set tool bar style.
Set whether toolbar displays text.
Indicates the options of Navigation's Menu.
More button options.
Indicates the options of Navigation's Menu.
Background blur style.
Background blur style options.
Background effect options.
Declare Navigation view properties.

Sets the width of the navigation bar. This attribute takes effect only when the **Navigation** component is in split mode.
Default value: **240**. Unit: vp.
**undefined**: No action is taken, and the navigation bar width remains consistent with the default value.
注释信息: @param { Length } value - Width of the navigation bar.

parameters.说明: Width of the navigation bar.

节点.功能描述:
Sets the position of the navigation bar. This attribute takes effect only when the **Navigation** component is split.
Default value: **NavBarPosition.Start**.
注释信息: @param { NavBarPosition } value - Position of the navigation bar.

parameters.说明: Position of the navigation bar.

节点.功能描述:
Sets the minimum and maximum widths of the navigation bar (effective in split mode).
Default value: 240 for the minimum value; 40% of the component width (not greater than 432) for the maximum value; if either of the widths is not set, the default value is used for that width.
Unit: vp.
注释信息: @param { [Dimension, Dimension] } value - Minimum and maximum widths of the navigation bar.

parameters.说明: Minimum and maximum widths of the navigation bar.

节点.功能描述:
Sets the minimum width of the navigation bar content area (effective in split mode).
Default value: **360**.
Unit: vp.
**undefined**: No action is taken, and the minimum width of the navigation bar remains consistent with the default value.
Breakpoint calculation in Auto mode: default 600 vp = minNavBarWidth (240 vp) + minContentWidth (360 vp).
注释信息: @param { Dimension } value - Minimum width of the navigation bar content area.

parameters.说明: Minimum width of the navigation bar content area.

节点.功能描述:
Sets the mode of navigation.
Default value: **NavigationMode.Auto**.
At the default settings, the navigation adapts to stack or split mode based on the component width.
注释信息: @param { NavigationMode } value - Display mode of the navigation.

parameters.说明: Display mode of the navigation.

节点.功能描述: Sets the icon of the back button in the title bar.
注释信息: @param { string | PixelMap | Resource | SymbolGlyphModifier } value - Icon of the back button in the title bar.

parameters.说明: Icon of the back button in the title bar.

节点.功能描述: Sets the back button icon and accessibility broadcast content. Default value: **back** when the system language is English.
注释信息: @param { string | PixelMap | Resource | SymbolGlyphModifier } icon - Indicates icon of back button
@param { ResourceStr } [accessibilityText] - Indicates content needs to broadcast.

parameters.说明: Indicates icon of back button
ResourceStr } [accessibilityText] - Indicates content needs to broadcast.

节点.功能描述:
Hide the NavBar, which includes title bar, the child of Navigation and tool bar. Supported in all mode. It will show top page in the NavPathStack directly or empty if there is no page in the NavPathStack.
Default value: **false**. **true**: Hide the navigation bar. **false**: Show the navigation bar.
注释信息: @param { boolean } value - Whether to hide the navigation bar.

parameters.说明: Whether to hide the navigation bar.

节点.功能描述:
Navigation title
When the NavigationCustomTitle type is used to set the height, titleMode does not take effect.
When the title string is too long:
If no subtitle is set, the string is scaled down, wrapped in two lines, and then clipped.
If a subtitle is set, the subtitle is scaled down and then clipped.
注释信息: @param { ResourceStr | CustomBuilder | NavigationCommonTitle | NavigationCustomTitle } value - Navigation title.
@param { NavigationTitleOptions } [options] - Indicates the options of titlebar.

parameters.说明: Navigation title.
NavigationTitleOptions } [options] - Indicates the options of titlebar.

节点.功能描述: Sets the subtitle of the navigation.

parameters.说明: Content of subtitle.

节点.功能描述:
Hide navigation title bar
Default value is **false**. **true**: Hide the title bar. **false**: Show the title bar.
注释信息: @param { boolean } value - Whether to hide the title bar.

parameters.说明: Whether to hide the title bar.

节点.功能描述:
Sets whether to hide the title bar and whether to animate the visibility change.
Default value is **false**. **true**: Hide the title bar. **false**: Show the title bar.
Default value is **false**.
**true**: Animate the visibility change. **false**: Do not animate the visibility change.
注释信息: @param { boolean } hide - Whether to hide the title bar.
@param { boolean } animated - Whether to animate the visibility change.

parameters.说明: Whether to hide the title bar.
Whether to animate the visibility change.

节点.功能描述:
Sets whether to hide the back button in the title bar. The back button is available only when titleMode is set to **NavigationTitleMode.Mini**.
Default value: **false**. **true**: Hide the back button. **false**: Show the back button.
注释信息: @param { boolean } value - Whether to hide the back button in the title bar.

parameters.说明: Whether to hide the back button in the title bar.

节点.功能描述:
Sets the display mode of the navigation title mode.
Default value: **NavigationTitleMode.Free**
注释信息: @param { NavigationTitleMode } value - Display mode of the page title bar.

parameters.说明: Display mode of the page title bar.

节点.功能描述:
Sets the menu items in the upper right corner of the navigation. If this attribute is not set, no menu item is displayed. When the value type is NavigationMenuItem, The menu shows a maximum of three icons in portrait mode and a maximum of five icons in landscape mode, With excess icons (if any) placed under the automatically generated **More** icon.
Menu items in the upper right corner of the navigation.
Navigation title bar's menus
注释信息: @param { NavigationMenuOptions } [options] - Indicates the options of menu.

parameters.说明: NavigationMenuOptions } [options] - Indicates the options of menu.

节点.功能描述: Sets the content of the toolbar. If this attribute is not set, no toolbar is displayed. Toolbar items are evenly distributed on the bottom toolbar, with text and icons evenly spaced in each content area. If any item contains overlong text and there are fewer than five items, the toolbar will reduce the text size progressively, wrap the text over two lines if necessary, and then clip the text to fit.
注释信息: @param { object | CustomBuilder } value - Content of the toolbar.

parameters.说明: Content of the toolbar.

节点.功能描述: Sets the content of the toolbar. If this attribute is not set, no toolbar is displayed.

@param { NavigationToolbarOptions } [options] - Indicates the options of toolbar.

NavigationToolbarOptions } [options] - Indicates the options of toolbar.

The value **true** means that the navigation bar is visible, and **false** means the opposite.
